title: Have Mercy on Us, Son of David
subtitle: Two blind men cry out on the road, and a mute man is freed from the demon that bound his tongue

stillness: Be still, and let the noise of the road fall away.
Two blind men are crying out in the dark. Let your own heart begin to cry with them.
The Lord is near to those who call upon Him.
gospel: As Jesus passed on from there, two blind men followed Him,
begging for mercy with a great cry, saying:
Have mercy on us, Son of David.
When He had come into the house, the blind men came to Him,
and He asked them whether they believed He could do this.
They said to Him: Yes, Lord.
Then He touched their eyes and said:
According to your faith be it done unto you.
And He charged them, saying: See that no one know it.
But going out, they spread His fame through all that land.
As they went out, behold, they brought Him a man who was mute, and possessed by a demon.
And when the demon was cast out, the mute man spoke.
Never was the like seen in Israel.
But the Pharisees said: By the prince of demons He casts out demons.
behold: See the two blind men: they do not come simply, but crying out with a great voice,
asking for nothing else but mercy.
They call Him Son of David, for that name seemed a name of honor.
He leads them apart, into the house, to heal them alone — away from the praise of the crowd.
And there, after they confess Him not only Son of David but Lord — the name of power —
Their eyes are opened, and the light of the body is given back as faith gives light to the mind.
Then, going out, behold, others bring Him a mute man, possessed by a demon —
one not mute by nature, but because the demon lay in wait and held his tongue.
First the demon is driven out; then the silenced tongue is loosed, and the man speaks again.
The crowds marvel; but the envious Pharisees, unable to deny the deed, twist it to the worse part.
consider: Consider how great is the faith of those who simply believe — it earns the gathering of so many gifts, and their increase from God.
For with the heart one believes unto righteousness, but with the mouth confession is made unto salvation.
He did not question them as though ignorant — He knew all things with certainty — but so that outward confession might be added to inward faith.
The confession of the mouth earned the touch of divine tenderness. O happy touch, upon which such great power follows!
He healed them not on the road but in the house, to teach us to flee the praise of men and the glory of vainglory.
For when the just man is praised to his face, he is scourged in his mind.
Yet the healed men could not be silent: out of memory of the grace, they became His heralds and evangelists.
God commanded silence not absolutely, but against vainglory — and what serves the salvation of others may rightly be told.

Behold a fourfold blindness in the eyes of the soul: error about His divinity, error about His humanity, malice that despises the divine glory, and presumption that does not see hell nor fear justice.
The mute man teaches that first the demon must be cast out, and then the other offices of the body return.
Hatred and envy make men interpret deeds to the worse part and pervert all judgment.
The Pharisees could not deny the power, so they slandered the work — calling what divine power wrought the art of magic.
prayer: Lord Jesus Christ, light of eternal brightness,
that I may never fall asleep in the death of the soul;
I may see all that must be done,
and, helped by that same grace, grow strong to fulfill what I have seen;
and from there tell out Your benefits, to Your glory and the good of others.
Open also, Lord, my mute mouth,
loosing my sins by the pouring-in of grace,
and freeing my tongue to Your praises;
that, receiving the grace of speech,
I may accuse myself, praise You as God,
build up my neighbor, and preach the truth. Amen.

latinJewel.latin: Confessio oris tactum meruit divinæ pietatis. Felix tactus, ad quem tanta sequitur virtus!
latinJewel.english: The confession of the mouth earned the touch of divine tenderness. O happy touch, upon which such great power follows!
